originalTitle: Why JEPI Investors Gave Up $3,379 in Five-Year Gains
summary: JEPI sells you monthly income. What it quietly sells away is your upside. Over the past five years, a $10,000 stake in the fund grew to roughly $14,479. The same $10,000 in plain S&P 500 exposure grew to roughly $17,858. That gap is the hidden cost of a fund marketed on its yield. What You’re ... Why JEPI Investors Gave Up $3,379 in Five-Year Gains
content:
JEPI sells you monthly income. What it quietly sells away is your upside. Over the past five years, a $10,000 stake in the fund grew to roughly $14,479. The same $10,000 in plain S&P 500 exposure grew to roughly $17,858. That gap is the hidden cost of a fund marketed on its yield.

JPMorgan’s headline price is the easy part. JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF ( NYSEARCA:JEPI ) carries a net expense ratio of 0.35%, with no fee waiver bringing it down from the gross figure of 0.35%. On a $10,000 position, that is $35 a year, every year, scraped off the top before a single distribution lands.

Compared to a low-cost dividend peer like Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF ( NYSEARCA:SCHD ), which charges roughly 0.06%, the fee gap alone is about $29 per $10,000 per year. Over 20 years on a $100,000 position, that is real money diverted to the issuer. The expense ratio, though, is the smallest part of the bill.

The Part the Factsheet Doesn’t Highlight

JEPI runs a covered-call overlay (it sells call options on its stocks for cash premium). That overlay caps gains when markets rally. The performance gap is clear: over the trailing year, JEPI returned 8.6% while the S&P 500 returned 24%. Over five years, JEPI gained 44.79% while the S&P 500 gained 78.58%. The forfeited upside dwarfs the 0.35% fee by an order of magnitude.

The income itself is less stable than the “monthly distribution” label suggests. In 2026, monthly payouts have swung from $0.34443 in February to $0.44761 in May, before sliding to $0.38921 in June. Look further back: distributions peaked at $0.6104 in December 2022 and bottomed at $0.2572 in August 2021. The “income” is option premium, and option premium shrinks when volatility shrinks.

Then comes the tax layer. A meaningful slice of those distributions is option premium taxed as ordinary income, not qualified dividends. For a retail holder in a taxable account, that means a higher annual tax bill on the income you actually receive, on top of giving up appreciation that would have been taxed only when sold, if ever. That is tax drag the factsheet does not score against the yield.

Two alternatives expose the gap. For straight large-cap exposure, SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust ( NYSEARCA:SPY ) gives you the index without an option overlay capping rallies. For dividend income from blue-chip payers like Bristol-Myers Squibb, Chevron, and Coca-Cola, SCHD delivered 24.11% over the trailing year and 54.75% over five years, with mostly qualified-dividend treatment. Neither replicates JEPI’s headline yield, and that is the point: you give up some monthly cash to stop paying the upside tax.

The overlap argument bites too. JEPI’s top holdings as of March 30, 2026 read like a tech index: NVIDIA at 7.9%, Apple at 6.4%, Alphabet at 6.4%, Microsoft at 5.0%, and Amazon at 4.8%. If you already own SPY or Invesco QQQ Trust ( NASDAQ:QQQ ), you are paying 0.35% to re-buy the same mega-caps, then paying again in capped upside when those names rip.

JEPI pays. The real question is what the yield costs in foregone appreciation, ordinary-income tax, and overlap with index funds you may already hold. Before adding more, look at your last brokerage 1099, compare your JEPI total return to SPY since you bought, and ask whether monthly cash is worth the gap.
chineseTitle: 为什么 JEPI 投资者放弃了 5 年收益 3,379 美元
chineseSummary: JEPI 向您出售月收入。它悄悄地卖掉的是你的优势。过去五年里，该基金价值 10,000 美元的股份增至约 14,479 美元。相同的 10,000 美元的标准普尔 500 指数敞口增加到大约 17,858 美元。这一差距就是以收益率营销的基金的隐性成本。你是什​​么......为什么 JEPI 投资者放弃了 5 年收益 3,379 美元
chineseContent:
JEPI 向您出售月收入。它悄悄地卖掉的是你的优势。过去五年里，该基金价值 10,000 美元的股份增至约 14,479 美元。相同的 10,000 美元的标准普尔 500 指数敞口增加到大约 17,858 美元。这一差距就是以收益率营销的基金的隐性成本。

摩根大通的总体价格是最容易的部分。摩根大通股票溢价收入 ETF ( NYSEARCA:JEPI ) 的净费用率为 0.35%，没有费用豁免使其低于 0.35% 的总费用。 On a $10,000 position, that is $35 a year, every year, scraped off the top before a single distribution lands.

与施瓦布美国股息股票 ETF (NYSEARCA:SCHD) 等收费约为 0.06% 的低成本股息同行相比，每年每 10,000 美元的费用差距约为 29 美元。 Over 20 years on a $100,000 position, that is real money diverted to the issuer. The expense ratio, though, is the smallest part of the bill.

情况说明书没有强调的部分

JEPI runs a covered-call overlay (it sells call options on its stocks for cash premium).当市场上涨时，这种叠加限制了收益。 The performance gap is clear: over the trailing year, JEPI returned 8.6% while the S&P 500 returned 24%. Over five years, JEPI gained 44.79% while the S&P 500 gained 78.58%. The forfeited upside dwarfs the 0.35% fee by an order of magnitude.

The income itself is less stable than the “monthly distribution” label suggests. In 2026, monthly payouts have swung from $0.34443 in February to $0.44761 in May, before sliding to $0.38921 in June.再往前看：分布在 2022 年 12 月达到峰值 0.6104 美元，并在 2021 年 8 月触底于 0.2572 美元。“收入”是期权溢价，当波动性缩小时，期权溢价会缩小。

然后是税收层。 A meaningful slice of those distributions is option premium taxed as ordinary income, not qualified dividends.对于应税账户的零售持有者来说，这意味着您实际收到的收入每年要缴纳更高的税款，此外还要放弃仅在出售时才需纳税的升值（如果有的话）。 That is tax drag the factsheet does not score against the yield.

有两种替代方案暴露了这一差距。 For straight large-cap exposure, SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust ( NYSEARCA:SPY ) gives you the index without an option overlay capping rallies.对于来自百时美施贵宝、雪佛龙和可口可乐等蓝筹股的股息收入，SCHD 在过去一年的股息收入为 24.11%，在五年内的股息收入为 54.75%，大部分采用合格股息处理。 Neither replicates JEPI’s headline yield, and that is the point: you give up some monthly cash to stop paying the upside tax.

重叠论点也很有效。截至 2026 年 3 月 30 日，JEPI 的最高持股看起来就像一个科技指数：NVIDIA 为 7.9%，苹果为 6.4%，Alphabet 为 6.4%，微软为 5.0%，亚马逊为 4.8%。如果您已经拥有 SPY 或 Invesco QQQ Trust（纳斯达克股票代码：QQQ），则您需要支付 0.35% 的费用来重新购买相同的大型股，然后在这些名称破裂时再次支付有限的上涨空间。

JEPI pays.真正的问题是，放弃升值、普通所得税以及与您可能已经持有的指数基金的重叠所带来的收益成本是多少。在添加更多之前，请查看您上次的经纪业务 1099，将您购买以来的 JEPI 总回报率与 SPY 进行比较，并询问每月现金是否值得这个差距。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
